title: galium elatum subsp. erectum
intro: Galium elatum subsp. erectum, also known as the alpine bedstraw or alpine woodbedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium elatum plant, a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Galium elatum subsp. erectum has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as acting as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent. Galium elatum subsp. erectum can be prepared into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile herb for herbal remedies.
